r.description: Number of miscarriages out of 10 pregnant women (possible values of the binomial random variable).

Probability.description: Binomial probabilities P(r) for each value of r with n=10 and p=0.15. The answer sums P(r) for r=6 through r=10 to find the probability that more than half (i.e., more than 5) have a miscarriage.

p.description: Probability of miscarriage for any single pregnancy (binomial parameter p).

n.description: Number of pregnant women in the group (binomial parameter n), used to define 'more than half' as more than 5.
